flowTitle: Visual Flow Creation and Code Injection Engine

contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped visual authoring and code-injection safety

directRuntimePeerDependencies.purpose: Code injection audit extends the non-repudiation audit model.

coreGuarantees: Canvas writes verify tenant ownership before state checks or writes.
Published flows are immutable and cannot be edited.
Publication validates cycles before type compatibility and uses OCC for the DRAFT to PUBLISHED transition.
Node type registration acquires SETNX before the dual write and releases locks on write failure.
Code injection takes a version lock before pre-write audit and injection.
Every state event preserves store-before-enqueue ordering.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-18 modifies generated executable flow behavior and requires package-signing first pass before Tier 3.

machineLockedRules: DFS cycle detection before type compatibility
type compatibility per edge before publish write
SETNX before node type and capability writes
node type record write before capability index write
redis.del lock cleanup on write failure

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-18 operating policy values. These values may vary by tenant without changing tenant isolation, immutable publication safety, lock ordering, audit ordering, or generated-code provenance.

keys.semantic: Maximum nodes allowed on a single visual flow canvas.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune canvas complexity while ownership, immutable-state, and audit order remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Maximum edges allowed on a single visual flow canvas.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune graph density while published-flow immutability remains MACHINE.

keys.semantic: Maximum graph traversal depth before publication validation aborts.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may support shallow or deep authoring graphs while DFS-before-type-check ordering remains fixed.

keys.semantic: Whether publication requires strict source-output to target-input schema matching.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may choose strict or permissive compatibility, but validation still runs before OCC publish.

keys.semantic: Maximum capabilities declared by one reusable node type.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune node catalogue richness while dual-write atomicity and lock cleanup stay fixed.

keys.semantic: TTL for the node type registration lock.

boundedRange.rationale: Lock duration may vary by registry throughput, but SETNX before both writes and redis.del cleanup remain MACHINE.

keys.semantic: TTL for a code-injection version lock.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune lock duration while version-lock-before-audit remains fixed.

keys.semantic: Maximum generated code payload size accepted for one injection.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune generated-code payload size while append-only audit and provenance remain fixed.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to flow-builder operating policy and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Enterprise automation studio
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A supports larger process maps and deeper validation traversals for internal platform teams.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T617/T618 still keep ownership, immutable-state, DFS ordering, type validation, OCC, and audit ordering fixed.

edgeCases.name: Strict production publisher
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A keeps strict type compatibility on before publication.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T618 still checks cycle detection before any compatibility decision and publishes through OCC only.

edgeCases.name: Rich node catalogue
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A allows more capabilities per reusable node type for an internal component marketplace.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T619 still uses SETNX before dual write and releases locks on write failure.

edgeCases.name: Large code injection workspace
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A permits larger generated code payloads for complex generated modules.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T620 still writes pre-injection audit before execution and appends every result audit.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T617 through T620 remain fixed.
Canvas BOLA verification must happen before state checks or writes.
DFS cycle detection must run before type compatibility checks.
Publication must use OCC for DRAFT to PUBLISHED transition.
Node type registration must acquire SETNX before node type and capability writes.
Node type registration must release locks on write failure.
Code injection must acquire a version lock before pre-write audit.
Pre-write audit must exist before injection code runs.
All state events preserve store-before-enqueue ordering.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through AsyncLocalStorage.

tenantAEnterpriseVisualStudio.description: Tenant A tunes the visual flow engine for enterprise automation authors.
tenantAEnterpriseVisualStudio.impact: Tenant A authors larger and stricter automation maps while isolation, publication immutability, lock ordering, and audit provenance stay unchanged.
tenantAEnterpriseVisualStudio.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: SETNX before node type dual write

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-18 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
